'''Jonny Vaughan''' (born 12 February 2005) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce|sr}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Salford RLFC|Salford]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on a one-week loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
He is a former 4 x amateur world champion kickboxer.{{cn|date=February 2026}}

==Club career==
===St Helens===
Vaughan spent his youth career playing for the St Helens academy team. 

In round 16 of the [[2024 Super League season]], Vaughan made his club debut for St Helens in their 8–6 loss against [[Castleford Tigers]]. 

===Swinton Lions (loan)===
During 2024, Vaughan also spent time on loan with Swinton in the [[RFL Championship]]. 

===Salford Red Devils (loan)===
On 29 March 2025, Vaughan joined the struggling Salford side on loan for the rest of the season after finding opportunities limited with St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.saintsrlfc.com/teams/first-team/jonny-vaughn/|title=Jonny Vaughan St Helens Profile|website=www.saintsrlfc.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp3y3k0l2vxo/|title=Salford bring in Saints youngster Vaughan on loan|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/24550235.jonny-vaughan-juggles-saints-duties-law-degree-studies/|title=Jonny Vaughan juggles Saints duties with law degree studies|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
On 21 September 2025, it was announced that Vaughan had signed a contract to join St Helens arch-rivals [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] ahead of the 2026 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c07vv5mgzvpo/|title=Wigan sign St Helens pair Vaughan and Sambou|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Rochdale Hornets (loan)===
On 12 February 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Feb 2026|accessdate=17 Feb 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=New Wigan Warriors recruit Jonny Vaughan lands short-term Championship loan move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jonny-vaughan-rochdale-loan-12-02-2026}}</ref>

===Salford RLFC (loan)===
On 5 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Salford RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Wigan Today|date=5 Mar 2026|accessdate=8 Mar 2026|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=The five Wigan Warriors youngsters featuring in Championship loan action this weekend|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/the-five-wigan-warriors-youngsters-featuring-in-championship-loan-action-this-weekend-5622167}}</ref>
